title: Cryptographic Randomness, Entropy, Keys, Salts, and Nonces · Language Lens: Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary
summary: A service seeds math/rand with time, generates keys and reset tokens from it, retries by reusing a nonce, and cannot prove fork or startup entropy behavior. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the salt token boundary increment in randomness and uniqueness contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Separate public unique salts, unpredictable bearer tokens, nonces, IVs, seeds, and secret keys by their exact guarantees.
Enforce construction-specific nonce uniqueness or misuse limits across concurrency, restart, rollback, and replication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary · Theory Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 5957 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to budget cryptographic randomness and nonce uniqueness across use, restart, and rollback; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
